growth_records.strain: SO01 (har-overexpressing engineered derivative of NG09, CGMCC No. 30951)
growth_records.medium: AMS (ammonia mineral salt; modified NMS with KNO3 replaced by NH4Cl)

up.method: peak carbon (methane) uptake rate from off-gas mass balance
qCH4 = mu_max/Y_XS = 0.156 h-1 / 0.97 gDCW/gCH4 = 0.1608 gCH4/gDW/h; /16.04 g/mol = 10.03 mmol/gDW/h
cumulative O2 uptake by integrating OUR; = 98.1 mmol O2/gDCW (520/5.30)

sec.method: no acids or growth-inhibiting by-products detected in broth (stated)

yields.Yxs: 0.97 g DCW / g CH4 (methane-based); 11.65 g DCW / g N (nitrogen-based)

growth_records.cite: Zixi Gao et al. Biological valorization of methane and nitrogen gas-derived ammonia via methanotrophic bacteria for gut-beneficial nutrients. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70448-6. [PMC13111605]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max 0.156 h-1 from Supplementary Table 2); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.156=4.44 h
growth_records.snippet: a maximum specific growth rate of 0.156 h-1 achieved... peak methane uptake rate (CUR), and highest DCW were 17.68, 33.60 mmol L-1 h-1, and 5.30 g L-1... methane- and nitrogen-based cell protein yields of 0.97 g DCW g-1 CH4 and 11.65 g DCW
growth_records.notes: Engineered SO01 (har-overexpressing) fed-batch on N2-derived ammonia, 3-L bioreactor. Reported: mu_max=0.156 h-1; max OD600=17.68; DCW=5.30 g/L; peak CUR(methane)=33.60 mmol/L/h; total CH4 uptake=5.47 g/L; cumulative O2=520 mmol/L; ammonia consumed=32.5 mmol/L (455 mg N/L). Normalized: 85.8 mg NH3-N and 98.1 mmol O2 per g cell protein (per g DCW: 455/5.30=85.8; 520/5.30=98.1). Yield_CH4=5.30/5.47=0.97 g DCW/g CH4. Doubling time calc: ln2/0.156=4.44 h. qCH4 derived approx from mu/Y=0.156/0.97=0.1608 gCH4/gDW/h=10.0 mmol/gDW/h (mu_max and yield not necessarily co-temporal, so approximate). pH controlled 7-8 (Methods) / 7-7.3 (Results text); 30±0.1 C. Productivity 147.2 mg cell protein/L/h.

growth_records.strain: SO01 (har-overexpressing engineered derivative of NG09, CGMCC No. 30951)

up.method: peak carbon (methane) uptake rate from off-gas mass balance

yields.Yxs: 0.94 g DCW / g CH4 (methane-based); 15.25 g DCW / g N (nitrogen-based)

growth_records.cite: Zixi Gao et al. Biological valorization of methane and nitrogen gas-derived ammonia via methanotrophic bacteria for gut-beneficial nutrients. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70448-6. [PMC13111605]
growth_records.method: not reported for nitrate condition (mu given only for ammonia run)
growth_records.snippet: the peak CUR was higher at 42.37 mmol L-1 h-1 in nitrate-utilizing bioprocess... accompanied by a total methane uptake of 7.42 g L-1 and a DCW of 6.94 g L-1. The corresponding methane- and nitrogen-based yields were 0.94 g DCW g-1 CH4 and 1
growth_records.notes: Nitrate-fed-batch comparator (same SO01 strain, matched 455 mg N/L). Reported: peak CUR(methane)=42.37 mmol/L/h; total CH4 uptake=7.42 g/L; DCW=6.94 g/L; Yield_CH4=6.94/7.42=0.94 g DCW/g CH4; Yield_N=15.25 g DCW/g N. No mu or doubling time reported for the nitrate run. Nitrogen-based yield of ammonia process (11.65) = 75% of nitrate (15.25). Methane yields nearly equivalent (0.97 ammonia vs 0.94 nitrate).
